Lookahead decoder with one extra future frame and a slightly lower move margin to better tolerate transition-frame misses in temporal move-execution ambiguity.
Lookahead decoder with one extra future frame but baseline move margin, isolating whether more settled context alone helps temporal move-execution ambiguity.
Lookahead decoder with baseline window but a slightly lower move margin, testing whether a small increase in move sensitivity helps temporal move-execution ambiguity.
Segmental decoder with the existing conservative sparse-event settings, testing whether piecewise-constant board segments reduce temporal move-execution ambiguity.
Lookahead decoder with a one-frame window and baseline margin, testing whether less future smoothing reduces temporal move-execution lag.
Lookahead decoder with the baseline window but a slightly higher move margin, testing whether more conservative move commits reduce temporal ambiguity desyncs.
Segmental decoder with conservative sparse-event settings plus one worst-frame drop per segment, testing whether suppressing transition frames helps temporal move-execution ambiguity.
Lookahead decoder with the baseline window and a slightly higher move margin, testing whether a milder conservative commit threshold helps temporal ambiguity without the larger m11 regression.
Lookahead decoder with the baseline window and a very slightly higher move margin, testing whether a smaller conservative shift than m10.5 can reduce temporal ambiguity without losing baseline fit.
Lookahead decoder with the baseline window and an even smaller move-margin increase, testing whether a near-baseline conservative shift can trim temporal ambiguity without the m10.25 regression.
Segmental decoder with conservative sparse-event settings but denser event proposals, testing whether allowing nearby candidate move frames helps temporal move-execution ambiguity.
Lookahead decoder with the baseline window and an ultra-small move-margin increase, testing whether the least-bad conservative direction helps only at a near-baseline threshold.
Segmental decoder with conservative sparse-event settings plus one board-evidence candidate, testing whether missed transition moves can be recovered without denser event proposal search.

Segmental decoder with one board-evidence candidate and a slightly higher move margin, testing whether the recall gain from board-backed transition recovery can be kept while reducing temporal over-commits.

Segmental decoder with one board-evidence candidate plus one worst-frame drop per segment, testing whether board-backed transition recovery can keep recall while suppressing transition-frame over-commits.
Segmental decoder with one board-evidence candidate and stricter temporal proposal spacing, testing whether board-backed transition recovery can help temporal ambiguity without nearby over-commits.

Segmental decoder with one board-evidence candidate and a higher board-change proposal threshold, testing whether board-backed transition recovery can keep temporal recall while pruning spurious event commits.
Segmental decoder with one board-evidence candidate and a stronger move margin, testing whether board-backed transition recovery can reduce temporal misses without the boardcands1 over-commit rate.

Segmental decoder with one board-evidence candidate, stronger move margin, and one worst-frame drop, testing whether board-backed transition recovery can keep temporal recall while further suppressing over-commits.
Segmental decoder with one board-evidence candidate and an even stronger move margin, testing whether board-backed transition recovery can keep temporal recall while suppressing more over-commits.

Segmental decoder with two board-evidence candidates and a stronger move margin, testing whether broader board-backed transition recovery can fix temporal ambiguity misses without reopening excessive over-commit search.

Segmental decoder with one board-evidence candidate, stronger move margin, and a smaller beam, testing whether board-backed transition recovery can reduce temporal misses without letting wrong trajectories persist.

Segmental decoder with one board-evidence candidate, stronger move margin, and a wider event window, testing whether more transition-frame context helps temporal ambiguity without broadening proposal search.

Segmental decoder with one board-evidence candidate, stronger move margin, and limited nearby secondary proposals, testing whether temporal ambiguity needs close alternate event frames without opening the full sep2 search.

Segmental decoder with one board-evidence candidate, stronger move margin, limited nearby secondary proposals, and one worst-frame drop per segment to test whether temporal ambiguity improves when transition frames are downweighted.

Segmental decoder with one board-evidence candidate, stronger move margin, and stricter nearby secondary proposals, testing whether temporal ambiguity benefits from close alternate event frames only when they are nearly as strong.

Segmental decoder with one board-evidence candidate, limited nearby secondary proposals, and a stronger move margin to test whether the v17 temporal-recall gain can be kept while trimming over-commits.

Segmental decoder with one board-evidence candidate, limited nearby secondary proposals, and a stronger move margin to test whether the v17 temporal-recall gain can be kept while trimming over-commits.

Segmental decoder with one board-evidence candidate, limited nearby secondary proposals, and a wider event window to test whether extra transition-frame context improves the strongest temporal-ambiguity branch.

Segmental decoder with one board-evidence candidate, limited nearby secondary proposals, and a slightly higher detect threshold to test whether the strongest temporal-ambiguity branch benefits from stricter event evidence.

Segmental decoder with one board-evidence candidate, limited nearby secondary proposals, and a looser secondary peak ratio to test whether slightly more close alternate event frames help temporal ambiguity.
Segmental decoder with one board-evidence candidate, limited nearby secondary proposals, and a slightly stronger move margin to test whether the v17 recall gain can be kept with fewer over-commits.

Segmental decoder with one board-evidence candidate, limited nearby secondary proposals, and wider primary event spacing to test whether the strongest temporal-ambiguity branch can cut spurious commits.

Segmental decoder with one board-evidence candidate, limited nearby secondary proposals, and fewer move candidates to test whether the strongest temporal-ambiguity branch over-commits due to move-search breadth.

Hybrid decoder keeps the baseline lookahead path unless a conservative board-only segmental pass earns a large sequence log-probability gain per extra committed move, targeting long temporal desync clips without inheriting segmental over-commits everywhere.

Hybrid decoder keeps the baseline lookahead path unless a conservative board-only segmental pass earns a slightly lower total sequence log-probability gain while keeping the per-added-move gate strict, targeting shorter temporal desync recoveries without reopening broad over-commits.

Hybrid decoder keeps the baseline lookahead path unless a conservative board-only segmental pass clears the lower total-gain gate and a slightly lower per-added-move gate, targeting shorter temporal desync recoveries that were previously blocked by the stricter move-normalized threshold.

Hybrid decoder keeps the baseline lookahead path unless a conservative board-only segmental pass clears the lower total-gain gate and a slightly lower per-added-move gate, targeting shorter temporal desync recoveries that were previously blocked by the stricter move-normalized threshold.

Hybrid decoder keeps the baseline lookahead path unless a conservative board-only segmental pass wins the current score-gain gates, while adding one state-aware proposal refinement pass to test whether missed transition events can be recovered without broadening the default search.

Hybrid decoder keeps the baseline lookahead path unless a conservative board-only segmental pass wins the current score-gain gates, while dropping one worst board frame per segment to test whether transition-frame noise can be reduced without giving up the existing hybrid gains.

Hybrid decoder keeps the baseline lookahead path unless a conservative board-only segmental pass wins the current score-gain gates, while widening the dropped-frame fallback to two board candidates to test whether a broader board-backed repair can recover temporal ambiguity clips without disturbing the cheap hybrid guardrails.

Hybrid decoder keeps the baseline lookahead path unless a conservative board-only segmental pass from the dropped-frame two-board-candidate fallback clears a stricter per-added-move gain gate, testing whether the v6 board_exact gain can be kept while pruning lower-quality temporal repairs.

Hybrid decoder keeps the baseline lookahead path unless a conservative board-only segmental pass from the dropped-frame two-board-candidate fallback clears a stricter total score-gain gate, testing whether the v6 board_exact gain can be kept while pruning shorter lower-value temporal repairs.

Hybrid decoder keeps the baseline lookahead path unless a conservative board-only segmental pass wins the current score-gain gates, while making the dropped-frame two-board-candidate fallback slightly more conservative on move commits to test whether the v6 board_exact gain can be kept with less collateral board churn.

Hybrid decoder keeps the baseline lookahead path unless a conservative board-only segmental pass wins the current score-gain gates, while making the dropped-frame two-board-candidate fallback only slightly more conservative on move commits to test whether the v6 board_exact gain can keep more recall while trimming some collateral board churn.

Hybrid decoder keeps the baseline lookahead path unless a conservative board-only segmental pass wins the current score-gain gates, while widening the dropped-frame fallback to three board candidates to test whether the v6 board-backed temporal repair still has headroom before the extra search starts hurting the guarded hybrid path.

Hybrid decoder keeps the baseline lookahead path unless a conservative board-only segmental pass wins the current score-gain gates, while making the dropped-frame two-board-candidate fallback stricter about nearby secondary proposals to test whether the v6 board_exact gain can be kept with fewer low-value temporal repairs.

Hybrid decoder keeps the baseline lookahead path unless a conservative board-only segmental pass wins the current score-gain gates, while requiring nearby secondary proposals in the dropped-frame two-board-candidate fallback to be at least as strong as the kept peak to test whether the v12 gain continues when close alternate events are pruned even harder.

Hybrid decoder keeps the baseline lookahead path unless a conservative board-only segmental pass wins the current score-gain gates, while requiring dropped-frame two-board-candidate fallback secondary proposals to be at least three frames apart to test whether the v12/v13 quality gain came from suppressing the closest alternate event frames.

Hybrid decoder keeps the baseline lookahead path unless a conservative board-only segmental pass wins the current score-gain gates, while disabling nearby secondary event proposals in the dropped-frame two-board-candidate fallback to test whether the v12/v13 quality gain persists when close alternate move-timing hypotheses are removed entirely.

Hybrid decoder keeps the baseline lookahead path unless a conservative board-only segmental pass wins the current score-gain gates, while keeping the stronger two-board fallback and stricter nearby-secondary gating but removing worst-frame dropping to test whether the v6 board_exact gain can keep the v12 quality gains without discarding useful transition evidence.

Hybrid decoder keeps the baseline lookahead path unless a conservative board-only segmental pass wins the current score-gain gates, while using a moderately stricter nearby-secondary gate in the dropped-frame two-board-candidate fallback to test whether the v12 quality gain still holds at the lightest active pruning threshold.

Hybrid decoder keeps the baseline lookahead path unless a conservative board-only segmental pass wins the current score-gain gates, while using the stronger nearby-secondary pruning from v12 and a smaller proposal budget to test whether the board_exact gain can be kept with fewer low-value temporal repair hypotheses.

Hybrid decoder keeps the baseline lookahead path unless a conservative board-only segmental pass wins the current score-gain gates, while keeping the stronger nearby-secondary pruning from v18 and shrinking the proposal budget again to test whether the board_exact gain still improves when low-value temporal repair hypotheses are pruned harder.

Hybrid decoder keeps the baseline lookahead path unless a conservative board-only segmental pass wins the current score-gain gates, while keeping the stronger nearby-secondary pruning from v18 and using a midpoint proposal budget to test whether the v18 gain survives with slightly less search than 16 proposals but without the v19 collapse at 12.

Hybrid decoder keeps the baseline lookahead path unless a conservative board-only segmental pass wins the current score-gain gates, while keeping the stronger nearby-secondary pruning from v18 and relaxing the proposal budget slightly to test whether useful distant temporal repair hypotheses were lost at 16 proposals.

Hybrid decoder keeps the baseline lookahead path unless a conservative board-only segmental pass wins the current score-gain gates, while restoring the stronger 16-proposal budget from v18 and slightly relaxing the nearby-secondary gate to test whether some useful close alternate event timings were pruned away at ratio95.

Hybrid decoder keeps the baseline lookahead path unless a conservative board-only segmental pass wins the current score-gain gates, while keeping the current strong 16-proposal shape and allowing even closer nearby-secondary events to test whether one-frame transition timing ambiguity is still being pruned away before hybrid selection.

Hybrid decoder keeps the baseline lookahead path unless a conservative board-only segmental pass wins the current score-gain gates, while keeping the current strong 16-proposal shape and slightly relaxing primary event spacing to test whether temporal repair needs denser move-timing hypotheses than min_event_separation=4 without reopening the known-bad sep2 search.

Hybrid decoder keeps the baseline lookahead path unless a conservative board-only segmental pass wins the current score-gain gates, while keeping the current strong 16-proposal shape and lowering only the total score-gain gate to test whether shorter temporal repair segments are being blocked despite still meeting the per-added-move quality bar.

Hybrid decoder keeps the baseline lookahead path unless a conservative board-only segmental pass wins the current score-gain gates, while keeping the current strong 16-proposal shape and lowering the per-added-move gain gate to test whether useful temporal repairs are being blocked by move-normalized scoring even after the lower total-gain gate proved inactive.

Hybrid decoder keeps the baseline lookahead path unless a conservative board-only segmental pass wins the current score-gain gates, while keeping the current strong 16-proposal shape and slightly lowering the segmental move margin to test whether missed transition-frame repairs still need a bit more move sensitivity now that recent hybrid-gate tweaks were inactive.

Hybrid decoder keeps the baseline lookahead path unless a conservative board-only segmental pass wins the current score-gain gates, while keeping the current strong 16-proposal shape and widening the segment event window to test whether transition-frame ambiguity needs a bit more local temporal context now that recent gate and margin tweaks were inactive.

Hybrid decoder keeps the baseline lookahead path unless a conservative board-only segmental pass wins the current score-gain gates, while keeping the current strong shape and slightly raising the proposal budget to test whether the active proposal-budget lever has additional board_exact headroom between the strong 16-proposal result and the worse 20-proposal run.

Hybrid decoder keeps the baseline lookahead path unless a conservative board-only segmental pass wins the current score-gain gates, while keeping the current strong shape and trimming the proposal budget by one from the weaker 18-proposal run to test whether the secondary-metric degradation starts immediately above the strong 16-proposal setting or only at higher search breadth.

Hybrid decoder keeps the baseline lookahead path unless a conservative board-only segmental pass wins the current score-gain gates, while keeping the new strong 17-proposal shape and restoring a slightly more conservative move margin to test whether the v30 non-empty and macro gains can be kept while trimming its false-change regression.

Hybrid decoder keeps the baseline lookahead path unless a conservative board-only segmental pass wins the current score-gain gates, while keeping the strong 17-proposal shape and splitting the move-margin difference between v30 and v31 to test whether some of v30's non-empty and macro gains can be kept without fully inheriting its false-change regression.

Hybrid decoder keeps the baseline lookahead path unless a conservative board-only segmental pass wins the current score-gain gates, while keeping the strong 17-proposal shape and bisecting the active move-margin transition between v32 and v31 to test whether the better non-empty and macro profile survives just below the apparent threshold where false-change improves.

Hybrid decoder keeps the baseline lookahead path unless a conservative board-only segmental pass wins the current score-gain gates, while keeping the strong 17-proposal shape and continuing the move-margin bisection just below 10.0 to locate the smallest conservative shift that preserves the stronger non-empty and macro profile.

Hybrid decoder keeps the baseline lookahead path unless a conservative board-only segmental pass wins the current score-gain gates, while keeping the strong 17-proposal shape and bisecting the newly narrowed move-margin boundary to locate the smallest conservative shift that still preserves the stronger non-empty and macro profile.

Hybrid decoder keeps the baseline lookahead path unless a conservative board-only segmental pass wins the current score-gain gates, while keeping the strong 17-proposal shape and taking one more move-margin midpoint between v46 and v57 to check whether any practical boundary remains before treating v46 as the usable strong-side setting.

Hybrid decoder keeps the baseline lookahead path unless a conservative board-only segmental pass wins the current score-gain gates, while keeping the strong 17-proposal shape and reverting to the last observed strong-side move margin after v54-v58 all landed on the same conservative profile, to confirm v46 as the practical usable setting before ending the float-boundary search.

Hybrid decoder keeps the baseline lookahead path unless a conservative board-only segmental pass wins the current score-gain gates, while holding the now-confirmed practical strong-side move margin from v59 and trimming the proposal budget back to 16 to test whether the stronger non-empty and macro profile depends on the 17th proposal or can be kept while recovering the lower false-change tradeoff of the older 16-proposal branch.

Hybrid decoder keeps the baseline lookahead path unless a conservative board-only segmental pass wins the current score-gain gates, while restoring the confirmed strong-side move margin and 17-proposal budget from v59 but tightening primary event spacing back to 4 to test whether the stronger non-empty and macro profile can be kept while trimming the extra false-change cost that appeared in the strong props17 branch.

Hybrid decoder keeps the baseline lookahead path unless a conservative board-only segmental pass wins the current score-gain gates, while restoring the cheap strong props17 shape after v61's no-op runtime spike and tightening nearby-secondary pruning from ratio90 to ratio95 to test whether the confirmed strong-side move margin can keep its non-empty and macro gains while dropping back toward the lower false-change tradeoff.

Hybrid decoder keeps the baseline lookahead path unless a conservative board-only segmental pass wins the current score-gain gates, while keeping the no-op-equivalent strong props17 shape from v62 and trimming move-search breadth from 16 to 12 candidates to test whether the confirmed strong-side move margin can keep its non-empty and macro gains while reducing spurious legal hypotheses behind the higher false-change profile.

Hybrid decoder keeps the baseline lookahead path unless a conservative board-only segmental pass wins the current score-gain gates, while taking the still-no-op strong props17 shape from v63 and trimming move-search breadth further from 12 to 8 candidates to find whether a real legal-hypothesis pruning threshold exists that can keep the stronger non-empty and macro profile while reducing the higher false-change cost.

Hybrid decoder keeps the baseline lookahead path unless a conservative board-only segmental pass wins the current score-gain gates, while splitting the new move-search breadth threshold between v63's no-op top12 result and v64's degraded top8 result to determine whether any practical pruning point exists before abandoning this lever as a way to reduce the strong props17 false-change cost.

Hybrid decoder keeps the baseline lookahead path unless a conservative board-only segmental pass wins the current score-gain gates, while keeping the no-op top10 strong props17 shape from v65 but restoring a stricter per-added-move gain gate to test whether stronger move-normalized hybrid selection can cut the extra false-change cost without giving up the stronger non-empty and macro profile.

Hybrid decoder keeps the baseline lookahead path unless a conservative board-only segmental pass wins the current score-gain gates, while keeping the no-op-equivalent strong props17 shape from v66 and trimming proposal budget from 17 to 15 to test whether a practical budget threshold exists between the degraded low-budget branch and the stronger 16-17 proposal regime.

Hybrid decoder keeps the baseline lookahead path unless a conservative board-only segmental pass wins the current score-gain gates, while keeping the new props15 branch from v67 but relaxing the per-added-move gate back to 125 to isolate whether the degraded 15-proposal profile comes from proposal budget itself or from the stricter move-normalized hybrid selection threshold.

Hybrid decoder keeps the baseline lookahead path unless a conservative board-only segmental pass wins the current score-gain gates, while keeping the new props15 branch from v67 but relaxing the per-added-move gate back to 125 to isolate whether the degraded 15-proposal profile comes from proposal budget itself or from the stricter move-normalized hybrid selection threshold.

Hybrid decoder keeps the baseline lookahead path unless a conservative board-only segmental pass wins the current score-gain gates, while restoring one proposal after v67-v68 showed the props15 branch degrades regardless of the per-added-move gate to test whether the practical proposal-budget threshold in the current ratio95/top10 branch sits exactly between 15 and 16.

Hybrid decoder keeps the baseline lookahead path unless a conservative board-only segmental pass wins the current score-gain gates, while restoring the confirmed strong props17 branch after v69 revalidated the 15->16 proposal threshold and trimming move-search breadth from 10 to 9 to test whether the remaining top-move cliff sits exactly between 8 and 10 and can keep the stronger non-empty and macro profile with fewer spurious legal hypotheses.

Hybrid decoder keeps the baseline lookahead path unless a conservative board-only segmental pass wins the current score-gain gates, while restoring the confirmed strong props17/top10 branch after v70 localized the move-search cliff at 9 and tightening only the total-gain gate to 150 to test whether a stricter whole-segment score requirement can trim the strong branch's extra false-change cost without giving up its non-empty and macro gains.

Hybrid decoder keeps the baseline lookahead path unless a conservative board-only segmental pass wins the current score-gain gates, while keeping the still-no-op-equivalent strong props17/top10 branch from v71 and raising only the board-change proposal threshold from 2/64 to 3/64 to test whether the strong branch's extra false-change cost is being driven by weaker board-change peaks that still survive the current proposal filter.

Hybrid decoder keeps the baseline lookahead path unless a conservative board-only segmental pass wins the current score-gain gates, while keeping the still-no-op-equivalent strong props17/top10 branch from v72 and raising only the detect proposal threshold from 0.1 to 0.15 to test whether the strong branch's extra false-change cost is being driven by weaker detect-driven proposal peaks after the board-change threshold increase proved inactive.

Hybrid decoder keeps the baseline lookahead path unless a conservative board-only segmental pass wins the current score-gain gates, while keeping the still-no-op-equivalent strong props17/top10 branch from v73 and dropping the two worst board frames per segment instead of one to test whether more aggressive transition-frame suppression can cut the strong branch's extra false-change cost without reopening the degraded low-budget or low-breadth regimes.

Hybrid decoder keeps the baseline lookahead path unless a conservative board-only segmental pass wins the current score-gain gates, while keeping the newly active drop2 branch from v74 but relaxing only the detect proposal threshold back to 0.1 to test whether the lower-false-change profile from dropping two worst frames can recover recall and board quality if detect-driven proposal breadth was over-pruned by the tighter 0.15 threshold.

Hybrid decoder keeps the baseline lookahead path unless a conservative board-only segmental pass wins the current score-gain gates, while restoring the stronger drop1 branch after v74-v75 showed drop2 itself drives the degradation and narrowing the event window from radius 2 to 1 to test whether less local temporal spread can reduce the strong props17 branch's extra false-change cost without giving up its best secondary metrics.

Hybrid decoder keeps the baseline lookahead path unless a conservative board-only segmental pass wins the current score-gain gates, while keeping the still-no-op-equivalent filtered props17 branch from v76 and raising only the segmental move margin to 10.0 to test whether this branch still has the known strong-to-conservative threshold and can recover the lower false-change tradeoff without a larger quality drop.

Hybrid decoder keeps the baseline lookahead path unless a conservative board-only segmental pass wins the current score-gain gates, while keeping the filtered props17 branch from v76-v77 and moving only to the first previously conservative-side move margin from the old props17 threshold search to test whether the narrowed event window and inactive proposal filters shifted the strong-to-conservative boundary or whether this branch remains threshold-identical to the original strong family.

Hybrid decoder keeps the baseline lookahead path unless a conservative board-only segmental pass wins the current score-gain gates, while restoring the strong-side move margin after v76-v78 showed the narrowed-window branch keeps the old threshold and trimming proposal budget from 17 to 16 to test whether proposal-budget activity is likewise unchanged by the inactive filter tweaks or whether the r1 branch shifts the 16-vs-17 tradeoff.

Hybrid decoder keeps the baseline lookahead path unless a conservative board-only segmental pass wins the current score-gain gates, while restoring the confirmed strong props17 branch after v79 showed the narrowed-window path preserves the old 16-vs-17 proposal tradeoff and shrinking the event window from radius 1 to 0 to test whether eliminating extra local temporal spread can reduce the strong branch's false-change cost without losing its best non-empty and macro profile.

Hybrid decoder keeps the baseline lookahead path unless a conservative board-only segmental pass wins the current score-gain gates, while keeping the still-no-op strong r0 branch from v80 and raising only the segmental move margin to 10.0 to test whether the zero-radius event window also preserves the known strong-to-conservative threshold and can recover the lower false-change tradeoff without a larger quality drop.

Hybrid decoder keeps the baseline lookahead path unless a conservative board-only segmental pass wins the current score-gain gates, while restoring the strong-side move margin after v80-v81 showed the zero-radius branch keeps the old threshold and trimming proposal budget from 17 to 16 to test whether proposal-budget activity is likewise unchanged when the segment event window is collapsed to a single frame.

Hybrid decoder keeps the baseline lookahead path unless a conservative board-only segmental pass wins the current score-gain gates, while keeping the zero-radius conservative branch from v82 and trimming proposal budget from 16 to 15 to test whether the known low-budget degradation also remains unchanged when the segment event window is collapsed to a single frame.

Hybrid decoder keeps the baseline lookahead path unless a conservative board-only segmental pass wins the current score-gain gates, while restoring the confirmed strong zero-radius branch after v82-v83 showed the low-budget degradation is unchanged and trimming board evidence from two candidates to one to test whether the second board candidate is still necessary in the constrained strong family or is now mainly contributing extra false-change opportunities.

Hybrid decoder keeps the baseline lookahead path unless a conservative board-only segmental pass wins the current score-gain gates, while restoring the confirmed strong zero-radius branch after v82-v83 revalidated the 16-vs-15 proposal-budget split and relaxing only the primary event spacing from 3 to 2 to test whether denser primary move-timing proposals can recover temporal ambiguity cases that the current secondary-peak mechanism still misses.

Hybrid decoder keeps the baseline lookahead path unless a conservative board-only segmental pass wins the current score-gain gates, while keeping the new strong zero-radius minsep2 branch from v85 and trimming proposal budget from 17 to 16 to test whether the additional timing density made the 17th proposal unnecessary or whether proposal-budget activity remains the main active lever in this improved branch.
